an3.10:1.1: “Monks, when one is endowed with three qualities, without abandoning three impurities, then as if one had been carried off, one would thus be placed in hell.
an3.10:1.3: One is unvirtuous and hasn’t abandoned the impurity of being unvirtuous.
an3.10:1.4: One is envious and hasn’t abandoned the impurity of envy.
an3.10:1.5: One is stingy and hasn’t abandoned the impurity of stinginess.
an3.10:1.6: When one is endowed with these three qualities, without abandoning these three impurities, then as if one had been carried off, one would thus be placed in hell.
an3.10:2.1: “Monks, when one is endowed with three qualities, having abandoned three impurities, then as if one had been carried off, one would thus be placed in heaven.
an3.10:2.3: One is virtuous and has abandoned the impurity of being unvirtuous.
an3.10:2.4: One is not envious and has abandoned the impurity of envy.
an3.10:2.5: One is not stingy and has abandoned the impurity of stinginess.
an3.10:2.6: When one is endowed with these three qualities, having abandoned these three impurities, then as if one had been carried off, one would thus be placed in heaven.”
